questions.question: A circle has center (3, −2) and passes through the point (7, 1). Write the equation of the circle in standard form.
questions.explanation: Find the radius using the distance formula: r = √[(7−3)² + (1−(−2))²] = √[16 + 9] = √25 = 5. The standard form is (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r².

questions.question: A sector of a circle has a central angle of 72° and radius 10. Find the arc length of the sector.
questions.explanation: Convert 72° to radians: 72° × (π/180°) = 2π/5 radians. Arc length = rθ = 10 × (2π/5) = 4π.

questions.question: A line is tangent to the circle x² + y² = 16 at the point (0, 4). What is the equation of the tangent line?
questions.explanation: At (0, 4), the radius is vertical, so the tangent line is perpendicular to it and horizontal. The equation is y = 4.

questions.question: A circle has radius 6. A sector of this circle has area 12π. What is the central angle of the sector in degrees?
The circle (x − 1)² + (y + 3)² = 20 has a chord at distance 2 from the center. Find the length of the chord.
questions.explanation: Use the chord-distance relationship: (half-chord)² + (distance)² = r². So c² + 4 = 20, giving c² = 16, so c = 4. The full chord length is 2c = 8.

questions.question: A circle has center at the origin and radius 5. Point P(3, 4) lies on the circle. Is the line y = 2x − 5 tangent to the circle? Explain briefly.
questions.explanation: Find the distance from (0, 0) to the line 2x − y − 5 = 0: d = |−5|/√(4+1) = 5/√5 = √5 ≈ 2.24. Since √5 < 5, the line intersects the circle, not tangent.
